cards.title: 🦆 A Birdwatcher\'s Paradise
cards.body: The Danube Delta is one of the largest and best-preserved deltas in Europe, home to over 300 species of birds. Millions of birds from Europe, Asia, and Africa come here to nest or rest during migration. It is especially famous for having Europe\'s largest breeding population of pelicans, particularly the Great White Pelican.

cards.title: 🌿 Unique Ecosystem
cards.body: The Delta is a maze of lakes, reed beds, channels, and islands. It holds the largest continuous marshland in Europe, and features Letea Forest, the northernmost subtropical forest in Europe, known for its wild horses, sand dunes, and lianas intertwined with ancient oak trees.

cards.title: 🛶 Traditional Villages
cards.body: The Delta is sparsely populated by traditional fishing communities. The Lipovans, descendants of Russian Old Believers who fled persecution in the 18th century, retain their traditional way of life, colorful wooden houses with thatched roofs, and distinct cuisine, heavily focused on fresh fish.

cards.title: 🌊 The Three Branches
cards.body: Before flowing into the Black Sea, the mighty Danube River splits into three main branches: Chilia (the northernmost, bordering Ukraine), Sulina (the middle branch, heavily dredged for navigation), and Sfântu Gheorghe (the southernmost and most meandering branch).

cards.title: 📍 Visitor Info
cards.body: The best way to explore the Delta is by boat, starting from the city of Tulcea or villages like Murighiol and Mahmudia. Small local boat tours offer the best chance to navigate the narrow channels and spot wildlife without disturbing the ecosystem. Bring plenty of mosquito repellent!
